environment.architecture: Rough-hewn stone and timber buildings along muddy tracks. Burned-out ruins at the eastern edge speak to a violent past.
environment.activity: Miners, farmers, and laborers move through town. Redbrand ruffians loiter near the Sleeping Giant, eyeing visitors.
environment.sensory: Smell of woodsmoke, horse dung, and fresh-baked bread. The creak of wagon wheels on packed earth.
environment.lighting: Lanterns lit at dusk; most of the town dark and quiet by the second hour of night.

curiosities.detail: Redbrand thugs at the Sleeping Giant watching strangers arrive.
Townspeople avoid the eastern end of town near Tresendar Manor.
Phandalin was sacked by orc raiders a century ago; it only recently resettled.

environment.activity: Travelers, miners, and off-duty workers drinking and eating. A fire crackles in the common room hearth.
environment.sensory: Smell of roasting meat, pipeweed smoke, and spilled ale. Murmur of quiet conversation.

curiosities.detail: Locals at the bar whispering about Redbrand trouble and disappearances.
Toblen shares rumors: Redbrands, Sildar, and the road dangers north.

environment.architecture: Large trading post, the biggest building in town. Shelves stocked with mining gear, trail rations, rope, lanterns, and basic equipment.
environment.activity: Miners picking up supplies before a shift; Elmar tallying ledgers behind the counter.

environment.architecture: Sturdy warehouse with a painted sign: a blue lion on white background. Armour and weapons displayed inside.
environment.activity: Linene Graywind inspects goods and greets customers with wariness.

environment.architecture: Large hall where miners register their claims and sell ore. Maps of local mine shafts cover one wall.
environment.activity: Halia Thornton sits behind a desk, politely but coolly observing everyone who enters.

environment.architecture: Small stone building dedicated to Tymora. Simple altar with a few coin offerings.
environment.sensory: Incense and the faint sound of wind chimes.

environment.architecture: Square stone building doubling as a jail (one crude cell in the back). A notice board outside lists wanted bounties.
environment.activity: Harbin Wester tries to look busy and avoid confrontation.

curiosities.detail: Notice board: 500 gp bounty for orc ears from Wyvern Tor.

environment.architecture: Dingy, low-roofed taphouse frequented by Redbrand ruffians. A half-ogre bartender glowers at the door.
environment.sensory: Stale beer, unwashed bodies, and the acrid tang of cheap tallow candles.

environment.architecture: Prosperous halfling farmstead on the south edge of town. Tidy herb garden, chickens pecking in the yard.
environment.sensory: Fresh earth, lavender, and woodsmoke from a small kitchen fire.

environment.architecture: Old apple orchard and cottage on the western edge of town. A retired adventurer's home, well-kept.
environment.sensory: Smell of apple blossoms or ripening fruit depending on the season.

environment.architecture: Burned-out ruins of an old manor on the eastern hill. Walls still standing, roof collapsed. Entry to the Redbrand Hideout below.
environment.sensory: Smell of old ash, rotting wood, and damp stone. The sound of distant dripping water.

curiosities.detail: A trapdoor hidden under debris leads to the cellar complex below.

environment.architecture: Underground cellar complex carved from natural rock. Crevasse with rickety bridge, a crypt, cells, and Glasstaff's wizard lab.
environment.sensory: Damp stone, mildew, distant sound of water below. The occasional groan from prisoners.

curiosities.detail: The nothic watches from the far side of the crevasse, sensing thoughts.
A letter from the Black Spider names Iarno as an agent and mentions the Lost Mine.

environment.architecture: Cave complex hidden in a hillside along Triboar Trail, overgrown with thorn bushes. Goblin lair with three main chambers.
environment.sensory: Stench of goblins, wet animal hide, and cookfire smoke. Sound of wolves chained in the dark.

curiosities.detail: A trip wire stretched across the path — connected to empty cans and pots.
Goblins at the cave mouth; sentries in the woods 90 feet back.

environment.architecture: Crumbling castle ruin in the Neverwinter Wood, occupied by the Cragmaw tribe. Seven main towers, roofs partially collapsed, hobgoblins at the entrance.
environment.sensory: Smell of unwashed creatures, straw, and old stone. Distant sound of drums and guttural argument.

curiosities.detail: Hobgoblin sentries in the gatehouse towers — alert and armed.
A map of Phandalin, with Tresendar Manor marked. A note in Undercommon from the Black Spider.

environment.architecture: Vast cavern system, flooded in sections. Waves of water crash against the rock walls in rhythmic pulses. Ancient dwarven and gnomish mine workings, now haunted by undead.
environment.sensory: The rhythmic boom of waves deep underground. Cold, damp air. Faint magical phosphorescence on the cave walls near the Forge of Spells.
environment.lighting: Near-total darkness except for the Forge of Spells' eerie green glow.

curiosities.detail: The flameskull patrols between rooms 18 and 19 on a fixed route.
The Forge of Spells still functions — enchantments can be applied to weapons and armor here.

environment.architecture: Ruined town at the foot of Mount Hotenow, destroyed thirty years ago when the volcano erupted. Half-collapsed buildings choked with twig blights and undead.
environment.sensory: Ash drifts on the wind. Unnatural silence broken by the rustle of dead vegetation. The distant creak of a dragon shifting in its tower.
environment.lighting: Perpetual grey overcast, ash filtering the light.

curiosities.detail: Fresh claw marks on stone — too large for any local creature.
The twig blights move in organised patterns as if directed by an unseen will.

environment.architecture: Ruined watchtower from the old empire of Netheril, in the hills northeast of Phandalin. A ring of twelve zombies shambles around the perimeter.
environment.sensory: Faint smell of decay. A Red Wizard's pavilion tent pitched beside the well — incongruously orderly amid the ruins.

environment.architecture: Rocky tor in the hills east of Triboar Trail. An orc war band has made camp in a cave on the southeast face.
environment.sensory: Reek of orc musk. Crude bone-and-hide totems around the entrance.

environment.architecture: Abandoned village, crumbling to rubble. Agatha's lair is a decrepit cottage deep in the nearby forest, its interior kept tidy by supernatural will.
environment.sensory: Silence. No birdsong. Inside Agatha's lair: the smell of old perfume and parchment, candles burning with cold blue flame.

environment.architecture: Rutted dirt road cutting through the Sword Coast wilderness, connecting Triboar to the coast. Infrequently traveled.
environment.sensory: Wind in the pines, birdsong, the occasional creak of a wagon. The road feels exposed — watchers in the trees are possible.
environment.weather: Highly variable; fog common in mornings near the river.
